title: C++ For Reverse Engineering
description: Modern C++ concepts essential for understanding compiled game code.

topics.title: Modern C++ Overview
topics.description: C++11/14/17 features seen in game codebases.
topics.explanation:
Modern game engines use C++17/20 features: smart pointers, lambdas, std::optional, constexpr, and RAII. Recognizing these patterns in decompiled output speeds analysis.

Key patterns:
- **RAII** — destructors clean up resources
- **Templates** — code bloat, mangled names
- **STL containers** — std::vector layout in memory
- **Move semantics** — std::move, rvalue references
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Modern C++ Overview
Study official documentation and recommended articles
Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment
Apply knowledge to a simple open-source game target
Document findings and review prerequisites for advanced topics
exercises: Write a summary explaining Modern C++ Overview in your own words
Create a diagram mapping key concepts and relationships
Complete one guided lab exercise related to this topic

topics.title: Pointers
topics.description: Pointer arithmetic and indirection in RE context.
topics.explanation:
Pointers are addresses. In assembly: `mov rax, [rcx]` dereferences RCX. Pointer arithmetic scales by type size.

RE skills:
- Identify pointer chains (base + offsets)
- Recognize null checks
- Trace pointer assignments in constructors
learningPath: Read the overview and core concepts for Pointers

Complete hands-on exercises in a controlled lab environment

topics.title: References
topics.description: C++ references vs pointers in compiled code.
topics.explanation:
References compile to pointer operations — often indistinguishable in optimized code. `T& ref = obj` becomes address-of and dereference operations.

Decompilers may show references as pointers. Understand both semantics.

topics.title: Classes & OOP
topics.explanation:
C++ classes compile to:
- **Vtable pointer** at object start (with virtual methods)
- **Member layout** following declaration order (with padding)
- **Inheritance** — base subobject at offset 0

Finding vtables in Ghidra/IDA reveals class hierarchies. RTTI helps identify types.

topics.title: Templates
topics.explanation:
Templates generate specialized code per type. Symbol names are **mangled** (e.g., `?push_back@?$vector@HV?$allocator@H@std@@@std@@`).

Tools: undname (MSVC), c++filt. Demangling reveals container types and function signatures.

topics.title: STL Containers
topics.explanation:
Common layouts (libstdc++/MSVC):
- **vector**: `{T* begin, T* end, T* capacity}`
- **string (SSO)**: inline buffer or heap pointer + size
- **map**: typically red-black tree nodes

Knowing layouts lets you interpret memory dumps and reconstruct structures.

topics.title: Memory Management
topics.explanation:
Games may override global new/delete or use pool allocators. Smart pointers (`unique_ptr`, `shared_ptr`) add control block overhead for shared ownership.

Track allocations in debugger to find object birth points.

topics.title: C++ Multithreading
topics.description: std::thread, mutexes, atomics in game code.
topics.explanation:
C++11 threading wraps OS primitives. `std::mutex` → CriticalSection/CRITICAL_SECTION on Windows. Atomics compile to LOCK-prefixed instructions.

Thread-local storage (thread_local) maps to TEB slots.

topics.title: WinAPI from C++
topics.description: Calling WinAPI and COM from C++ games.
topics.explanation:
Games use WinAPI directly and via wrappers. COM interfaces (DirectX, audio) use vtable calls: `mov rax, [rcx]; call [rax+offset]`.

Practice: trace CreateWindowEx, D3D11CreateDevice in a sample app.
